sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient presented with a history of a right knee injury sustained 3 days ago during a soccer game, where he felt a pop in his knee while trying to kick the ball.
He reported swelling, pain, and limited function of the knee, which has not improved with anti-inflammatory medication.
He has noticed a marked limp on his right leg and has difficulty bending and straightening his knee, especially at the end of the motion.
Past medical history: [NOT MENTIONED]
Review of systems: [NOT MENTIONED]
Current medications: I prescribed ibuprofen for pain relief.
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient had a marked limp on his right leg upon ambulation.
There was significant effusion in the right knee with slight ecchymosis.
Palpation of the medial joint line caused pain.
Passive range of motion revealed difficulty with terminal extension to 115 degrees and terminal flexion to 70 degrees due to pain.
The Lachman exam was positive.
Muscle strength was 5/5 in both lower extremities, and neurovascularly, the bilateral lower extremities were intact.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient's right knee injury is suggestive of an anterior cruciate ligament (ACL) tear.
Problem list: Anterior cruciate ligament tear; suspected; confirmed.
Plan:
I ordered an MRI to evaluate the integrity of the ACL.
I prescribed meloxicam 15 milligrams daily for pain and swelling.
I advised the patient to ice the knee if necessary and to use a knee immobilizer and crutches to reduce stress and strain on the knee.
I instructed the patient to limit activities, including work activities, and to avoid jogging until the results of the MRI are available.
I recommended moderate activities to avoid locking of the knee.
Follow-up: The patient will be scheduled for an MRI, and I will review the results with him to determine the next course of action.
